title: pilea angulata subsp. petiolaris
intro: Pilea angulata subsp. petiolaris, also known as Pilea angulata 'Petio', is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Urticaceae. It is native to tropical regions and is commonly used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating digestive issues, when used in herbal preparations. In traditional herbal medicine, Pilea angulata subsp. petiolaris is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its extracts are used in various forms of herbal supplements.
